bibliographic_data.title: Can TM system form an unconditional basis for Banach spaces?
bibliographic_data.abstract: The research on the algorithm of analytic signal has received much attention for a long time. Takenaka-Malmquist (TM) system was introduced to consider analytic functions in 1925. If TM system satisfies hyperbolic inseparability condition, then it is an orthogonal basis. It can form unconditional basis for Hilbert space H2(D) and Schauder basis for Banach space Hp(D)(1 < p < ∞). In characterizing a function space, a necessary condition is whether the basis is unconditional. But since the introduction of TM systems in 1925, to the best of our knowledge, no one has proved the existence of a TM system capable of forming an unconditional basis for Banach space Hp(D)(p ≠ 2). In this paper, we prove that two TM systems can form unconditional basis for Hp(D)(1 < p < ∞).

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper proves that two Takenaka-Malmquist (TM) systems can form an unconditional basis for Banach space Hp(D)(1 < p < ∞).
core_content.key_methodology: The authors construct specific TM systems and prove their properties, including the vanishing moment, locality, distance to boundary, and rotation of the argument.
outcomes: Proved that two TM systems can form an unconditional basis for Banach space Hp(D)(1 < p < ∞).
Solved a longstanding open problem in the field.
core_content.full_text_markdown: The paper investigates whether Takenaka-Malmquist (TM) systems can form an unconditional basis for Banach spaces. It is known that TM systems are orthogonal and Schauder bases, but their unconditional properties have not been established for p ≠ 2. The authors construct specific TM systems and prove their properties, including the vanishing moment, locality, distance to boundary, and rotation of the argument. They show that two such systems can form an unconditional basis in Hp(D)(1 < p < ∞), solving a longstanding open problem.
